purpose: One option in a mutually exclusive group: a light-DOM element wrapping a real <input type="radio"> inside its own label. Radios sharing a name form a native group — mutual exclusion, arrow-key navigation, and form submission come from the platform.
useWhen: Exactly one choice among a small set of mutually exclusive options, all visible at once.
The choice is submitted with a form rather than taking effect immediately.

notWhen.case: The setting takes effect immediately, with no form submit.
The option list is long or space is constrained.

guidance: Radios only behave as one group when they share a `name` — the group, not each radio, is the single form control.
A group should have a group label (a <fieldset>/<legend> or aria-labelledby on a wrapping group) in addition to each radio's own label.

default.description: The label content (the accessible name via the wrapping label).

checked.description: Indicator content shown while selected; replaces the drawn dot. Any markup: svg, icon-font glyph, HTML character, image.
guidance: The control is aria-hidden — the state is conveyed by the native input, so indicator content is purely visual.

unchecked.description: Indicator content shown while not selected.

keyboard.does: Moves the group's selection to the next / previous radio (native behavior, preserved).
The group is a single tab stop; focus lands on the selected radio (native).

examples.title: Basic group
examples.why: The shared name is what makes them one control; checked marks the group's current selection.

examples.title: Custom indicator
examples.why: Indicator content layers over the aria-hidden control, sized by indicator-size and colored by indicator-color.

examples.title: Lone radio
examples.why: A radio without group siblings can never be unchecked once selected — an on/off choice is zbk-checkbox.
